title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Inspector Hakast conducts several interviews following the murder of Ina Brent, a nineteen-year-old stenographer found strangled in a telephone booth. After Miss Pembroke denies seeing the girl or any suspicious activity at 19 Wilbraham Crescent, Hakast visits Miss Wutherspoon. Miss Wutherspoon claims she discovered the body while attempting to use the phone booth because her own phone was making a strange buzzing noise. When questioned about reports of the girl visiting her front gate, Miss Wutherspoon suggests the girl might have been delivering a flyer or perhaps was actually visiting Miss Pembroke, noting that a stenographer had reportedly visited Miss Pembroke on the day a man was killed. Hakast suspects Miss Wutherspoon may be attempting to deflect suspicion toward number 19.

Hakast then visits the Cavendish Typewriting Agency to speak with Miss Sheila Webber. He is met by Miss Martin, who berates him for the lack of police protection. Miss Martin argues that someone with a "perverted psychology" is targeting shorthand typists, noting that Miss Webber had been previously traumatized by being sent to discover a corpse, and now Ina Brent has been murdered. Miss Martin expresses concern that these killings are part of a "mad scheme" and mentions rumors regarding clocks, though Hakast dismisses these rumors as a matter of trial postponement.
